entry.title: Employee Confidential Profile in SAP SuccessFactors Built Using Story Reports
entry.content:
In today’s data-driven talent landscape, succession planning is no longer just about identifying successors — it’s about making informed, strategic decisions based on comprehensive employee insights.

One of the most powerful ways to enable this is by creating an Employee Confidential Profile using Story Reports in SAP SuccessFactors. This report consolidates critical talent data from multiple modules into a single, secure, executive-ready view designed specifically for succession discussions.

Overview

The Employee Confidential Talent Profile is a centralized Story Report built for Succession Planning that pulls data from multiple SuccessFactors sources, including:

  • Employee Photo
  • Employee Talent Profile
  • Performance & Potential Ratings
  • Assessment Data
  • Learning History
  • Development Goals
  • Talent Pool Nomination
  • Succession Nomination
  • Nominated Successors
  • Competency Data including self and manager rating

This report is typically used by:

  • HR Business Partners
  • Talent Management Teams
  • Leadership during Talent Review & Calibration sessions

It provides a 360-degree view of an employee’s readiness, capability, and growth trajectory — all in one structured and confidential format.

Key Features

Consolidated Talent Overview

The report combines multiple datasets into one profile view:

  • Current Role & Department
  • Tenure & Career History
  • Key Competencies
  • Risk of Loss
  • Impact of Loss

This eliminates the need to navigate across multiple modules.

Performance & Potential Trend Analysis

  • Performance and Potential ratings over time
  • Trend indicators (improving, stable, declining)

This allows leaders to analyze growth consistency and leadership trajectory.

Assessment & Readiness Data

  • Readiness levels (R1, R2, R3)
  • Successor nomination status
  • Assessment results
  • Talent flags (High Potential, Future Leader, etc.)

This is critical during succession slate discussions.

Learning & Development Insights

  • Completed learning courses
  • Certifications
  • Ongoing training programs
  • Individual Development Goals
  • Development Goal progress %

This helps evaluate whether the employee is actively closing competency gaps.

Employee Picture & Confidential Layout

Including the employee picture enhances:

  • Visual recognition during talent reviews
  • Engagement in calibration discussions

Since it is a confidential report, it includes:

  • Role-based permissions
  • Restricted access
  • No export rights (if required)

Benefits

Single Source of Truth

Brings together fragmented talent data into one powerful profile.

Better Succession Decisions

Leaders can evaluate readiness based on historical trends, not just current ratings.

Time Efficiency

Reduces manual preparation of talent review decks.

Improved Talent Transparency

Supports objective discussions backed by data.

Configuration Approach

Below is a structured approach to configure the Employee Confidential Profile using Story Reports:

Step 1: Enable Required Domains in Story Reporting

Ensure access to relevant schemas:

  • Employee Profile
  • Performance Management
  • Succession
  • Learning
  • Goal Management
  • Development Planning

These domains must be enabled in Manage Data → Report Schemas.

Step 2: Create a Cross-Domain Story

  1. Go to Reporting → New → Story
  2. Select Domain
  3. Add required domains:
    • Job Information
    • Performance Management
    • Succession Planning
    • Learning
    • Goal Management
    • Development Planning

Establish joins using:

  • User ID
  • Person ID External

Step 3: Build Report Sections

Design the profile in structured sections:

Section A – Employee Snapshot

  • Name
  • Job Title
  • Department
  • Manager
  • Photo
  • Tenure

Section B – Performance & Potential History

  • Performance rating (last 3 years)
  • Potential rating

Section C – Succession & Readiness

  • Critical Position indicator
  • Successor nominations
  • Readiness level (R1/R2/R3)
  • Risk of Loss

Section D – Learning & Development

  • Completed courses
  • Certifications
  • Development goals
  • Goal completion %

Step 4: Apply Confidentiality Controls

  • Restrict via Role-Based Permissions (RBP)
  • Control target population
  • Restrict export if required
  • Limit drill-down capability

Typically, access is granted only to:

  • HRBPs
  • Talent Managers
  • Senior Leadership

Step 5: Add Dynamic Filters

Enable filtering by:

  • Department
  • Business Unit
  • Location
  • Talent Pool
  • Critical Position flag

This allows live talent review meetings without multiple versions of the report.

Best Practices

  • Keep the design executive-friendly (avoid too many charts).
  • Use conditional formatting for readiness & performance ratings.
  • Highlight critical position successors separately.
  • Include performance trend rather than single-year rating.
  • Test data joins carefully to avoid duplication from multiple modules.

Final Thoughts

The Employee Confidential Profile built using Story Reports transforms succession planning from a manual, slide-based process into a real-time, analytics-driven experience.

By integrating data from:

  • Talent Profile
  • Performance & Potential History
  • Assessment Data
  • Learning History
  • Development Goals

Organizations can move toward evidence-based succession decisions rather than subjective evaluations.

In a competitive talent market, having the right successor ready at the right time is not optional — it’s strategic.

If you’re working on Succession Planning enhancements in SuccessFactors, this is one of the most impactful reports you can implement.

entry.title: Linking Process Owner Attributes from Process Manager to Automate Approver Assignment in Governance
entry.content: Can I link the Process Owner attribute defined in my Process Manager diagram to Process Governance, so that Governance can automatically assign the approval task to that Process Owner before the process is published to the Collaboration Hub?

entry.title: Re: Support of a Compact Display for Tables (Live BW)
entry.content:
Hello @GregBotticchio,

Cool feature which is appreciated to have it for BW live connection! 😊 Are there any plans to offer compact display for Datasphere live connection, too?

If not I'll create an influence item. I'm pretty sure other customers will ask for this feature for Datasphere live connection, too.

Thanks in advance for your reply!

Best regards

Lothar

entry.title: Re: How to calculate measures on property level and copy it to lowest dimension level
entry.content:
HI @SDMS90 ,

Apart from the solution posted above using data action, you can achieve this by model level calculation. 
for data persists in model , you can use the data action solution.

entry.title: Re: RITA - Tax Ctry/Reg in standard reports
entry.content: Use Advance Return for Tax on Sales/Purchases for compliance reporting. For internal analysis, consider building a Custom Analytical Query that includes Tax Country/Region from the underlying CDS views. If you need this dimension in standard apps (like Display Line Items), check if it’s available in the data source. If not, you’ll need CFL or custom reporting.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Learning - Discussions
entry.content:
hi @manpaul 

Can you give us a bit more information as to what you SF instance set up is like/includes (e.g. IAS, EC or just BizX/Platform etc..) and what exactly you are struggling with in the external users learning set up (User Profile, Sites, LMS to IAS replication or even IAS access provisioning)?

Regards,

Nathan H.

entry.title: Depreciation Key for Mexico
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

As per local requirements in Mexico, it is required that the depreciation is needed to be calculated from following month of acquisition instead of same month of Acquisition. Can you please check and suggest the correct depreciation key to be used. Thanks for your help on this.

Regards,

S. Sumana

entry.title: Texts in Purchase Contract Output Form not Displayed
entry.content: We have implemented SAP note 3401952 and added two new standard long texts for printing. Those are - A05 Terms of Delivery and A06 Shipping Instructions. However, neither of texts are available in the source for showing on the form. What we might be missing?

entry.title: Re: Oracle12c Database Installation steps
entry.content:
Hi @brindhavan,

Thanks for the blog

entry.title: Re: Change of declarant for VAT reporting
entry.content:
Good day @PlamenTraychev 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, the declarant data used in Run Statutory Reports is maintained in the statutory reporting configuration activity SSCUI 101071 under the Reporting Entity and the report activity properties.

Here are links to guide you: KBA 3287422, Setting Up the VAT Declaration - Monthly Report, Setting Up the Report and KBA 3453645

Note that 1010171 SSCUI ID is scope dependent. You might not be able to see and do the configure for setting up your statutory reporting in CBC if scope item 1J2 is missing in your tenant. You will need to open a case for item XX-S4C-OPR-SRV for that item to be activated.

Let me know if you still need a step by step guide.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to add F4 in Standard Fiori Apps for extended SAP Standard fields
entry.content: @Abh_2  : https://me.sap.com/notes/3618997/E - I think this note should help you , it seems like it addresses the exact issue you have Please implement in your system and check

entry.content:
Hi,

Please check whether it is in released and below mentioned options are selected.

BR,

Kasi

entry.title: Re: Header Billing Plan net value is 0
entry.content:
Mass Maintenance of Billing Plans: Instead of setting up 20+ items manually, you can use Mass Change tools in the GUI (transaction VBOF or via “Change Sales Orders” with multiple selection). Custom Fiori extensions or Custom CDS queries to update billing plans in bulk.

Copying Billing Plans: Create one item with the correct billing plan, then copy it across items using GUI functions or custom enhancements.

SAP Roadmap Check: SAP has been gradually enhancing Fiori apps. If header-level billing plan support is critical, it’s worth checking the latest release notes or raising an influence request with SAP.

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
Thanks as always @Marian_Zeis for your thoughts; it is perfectly fine to disagree - like I said in my post, we are all adults here, and disagreements are often how we learn and grow too. I understand your frustration and giving up the fight is not an unusual path to take, I get it, and the community is bigger than just the content on any given platform - for example, publishing on one's own blog (like you and I do) is a great way to grow oneself and find one's own voice, and grow the community as a whole too.

Beyond encouraging folks to acknowledge good posts, my primary concern is doing something about the avalanche of moderator reports that I think are (a) inappropriate and (b) causing such a load on @AnnePetteroe & the team which means they have no time to do anything else.

Even if some of the community start doing this, it may make a difference. Turning the giant cargo ship is no mean feat, and will take a while. But I think it's worth a try.

entry.title: Re: SAP Security Configuration Recommendations

Best Regards,
Toni

entry.title: Re: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP Platform Automation - February Edition
entry.content:
@DinoPhilips You are not late at all. There is no way to pre-subscribe to future session due to the fact that we have to set up the registration for every edition and a self-sign-up is required then. 
So how to handle this best:

  • Follow me here on the SAP Community as I will post the announcement here
  • Follow me on LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/in/christian-lechner-inthecloud/) as I will post the announcement there. In addition I post other news around btp CLI, Terraform and IaC in general there.

Not a super conveniant solution, but the only way to stay informed for these kind of events (as for the other roundtables like ABAP)

entry.title: Password self service in GRC Access control is not working for the lower case user ids.
entry.content:
Hello - We have an issue where password self service in GRC Access control is not working for the users who are having their user ids in the lower case. It gives error after selecting the target system for password "User ID xxxx does not exist". Our GRC system is recently upgraded to SP29 and post that this issue has occurred.

Can someone please help here.

entry.title: Where is "Z_I_BOOKING_000" defined?
entry.content: Enhance an ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) View in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) ABAP Environment − Step 9 Specify hierarchy − Where is "Z_I_BOOKING_000" defined in 1.? Also, where should "define view" be defined in 6.?

entry.title: Re: Change of declarant for VAT reporting
entry.content: Hello Chris, there is no message.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP AI best practices - SAP Document AI
entry.content: This is a public forum, but the event is for partners only.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Document Adjustment - most common mistakes and questions
entry.content:
To add to this blog: if you're using Version Management for your Purchase Orders, which in turn triggers the release strategy (in our case Flexible Workflow), Automatic Document Adjustment (ADA) is creating but not completing the newest version. This would then still be a manual task after running ADA. 

The most straight forward approach seems to be to execute this automatic document adjustment job with a next job step along, which should read the spool (PO result list) of the previous job step, then via a BAPI or other solution to complete the versions of those POs. But it's still a hassle.

Vish

entry.title: Building multitenant SaaS solutions with SAP Build: A partner's guide to innovate for scale
entry.content:
Building a SaaS solution is exciting.  Building one that scales cleanly across customers without breaking the core?  That’s where things get interesting.

In our upcoming webinar, “Building multitenant SaaS solutions with SAP Build: A partner's guide to innovate for scale,” we’ll walk through how you can design, deploy, and commercialize multitenant applications on SAP Business Technology Platform while staying true to clean core principles with SAP Build.

We’ll cover the real journey, from architecture and ERP-agnostic design to pricing models, provisioning, and cost structures for dev, test, demo, and production.  Plus, we’ll connect the dots between technical decisions and commercial impact.

Honestly, multitenancy isn’t just a technical choice.  It’s a business multiplier.  One code line.  Multiple customers.  Controlled extensions.  Stronger margins.

Whether you’re in the SAP PartnerEdge Open Ecosystem or a Build partner refining your portfolio, this session is designed to give you clarity and confidence.

Register now and start building for scale, the smart way.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013076.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPBuild #SaaS #SAPPartners #CleanCore

entry.title: Re: Triggering SAP Datasphere/BDC Task Chains from SAP BTP ABAP Cloud using the Task Chain REST API
entry.content:
Hi Niroshini,

yes indeed. It's possible.

You typically deal with the following classes in your implementation and you've created an SM59 HTTP connection for the API endpoint.

Important ABAP classes:

CL_HTTP_ENTITYHandle request/response body and headers
/UI2/CL_JSONJSON serialization/deserialization
CL_ABAP_CODEPAGECharacter encoding conversions
CL_HTTP_UTILITYURL encoding and utility functions
CL_HTTP_CLIENTHTTP Client/Request handling

Best regards, Stefan

Giovanni Olivieri

entry.title: Re: Designing a Multi‑Tenant Solutions on SAP BTP – Architecture and Prototype Walkthrough
entry.content: Nice blog. But you skipped the tenant isolation in the database, which is not really ensured with the service manager and a service-instance schema. This schema cannot be managed by the database independently from other schemas. That is a real gap in your solution. The right way is using the HANA Cloud native multitenancy capability, with customer-specific encrypted database tenants (containing schemas or HDI containers), resource control and independent lifecycle.   For native database tenants you use the Tenant Management Service instead of the Service Manager. See here for more details: https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-multitenancy/sap-hana-cloud-multitenancy?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: VA01 Item Overview Sorting
entry.content: How about through user exits?

entry.title: Re: How to make a field mandatory or warning trigger for...
entry.content:
Dear @jerrysam,

Option A: Recommended

Use Cloud Badi MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD (Check Invoice Data) to raise a W (warning) or E (error) message when the header KSeF field is empty. This is the same mechanism SAP recommends to enforce supplier-invoice checks in Public Cloud

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3673139

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/5a27c5f57c3f4dffa6881ca587b22571.html

Steps to do:

Option A:

1. Make sure the field exists on the invoice header:

  • If you already have a standard KSeF reference number/KSeF field available for supplier invoices use it SAP provides configuration/support for maintaining KSeF reference number in supplier invoices
  • If you don’t have it, create a custom field in Custom Fields and Logic with business context publish it and add it to the UI key user extensibility / Adapt UI. Available fields you can find in Badi below:

 

2. Go to Custom Logic

  • Create a new implementation for Badi: MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD

 

3. Implement the check

  • Use the ACTION parameter to decide when to show/stop:
    • Use action CHECK if you want users to see the message while entering the invoice when they press Check/Simulate
    • Use action POST / SAVE AS COMPLETED if you want to block posting when KSeF is missing
  • If the KSeF field is initial/blank append a message to the provided MESSAGES table:
    • Warning: message type W (allows posting, but warns)
    • Error: message type E (prevents posting)

For example:

    " Example 4: Issue an warning message

    " Only issue the message if YOUR FIELD is not filled

    " This message will not prevent any actions, however it will be displayed to the user

    " Please note that warning, success and information messages will only be displayed during the check action

    IF headerdata-YOURFIELD IS INITIAL AND

       action = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_action_check.

      APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_messagetype_warning

                     messageid = 'MRM_BADI_CLOUD'

                     messagenumber = '001'

                     messagevariable1 = YOUR FIELD is not filled.'  ) TO messages.

 

    ENDIF.

More details you will find inside Badi clicking:

 

4. Activate the implementation and test in Create Supplier Invoice Advanced (MIRO)

Option B:

If SAP already issues a standard message for your scenario and you only want to switch it between I/W/E you can use in CBC ID 102091 Define Message Types for Area M8

 

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2654157

IMPORTANT: You cannot change SAP predefined standard message type entries only customer-specific settings are possible and some changes are restricted by design so in my opinion option A is suitable for your case

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: GL Account not available as Preconditition for Market...
entry.content:
Good day @JavierIbanez 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is not a “forgotten field”. In Manage Solution and Validation Rules, the available precondition fields are restricted based on the business context. Market Segment context derives or validates profitability segment fields and only some drivers are exposed. See Manage Substitution/Validation Rules and KBA 2892205 for more details.

So, it is rather a scope and field exposure limitation of that specific business context in the key user app.

Regarding the availability of GL Account being available in Custom Logic BAdI, this often happens because Runtime structure has more fields and key user rule UI exposes only stable subset. That is based on the clean core key user tools design.

  • If your substitution must be driven by GL account, the clean approach I recommend is to keep the Market Segment rule simple or skip the rule app for this case. Implement the condition and substitution in FINS_MARKET_SEGMENT_SUB. This will help you avoid creating and populating a ZZ_GLACCOUNT like workaround field.
  • Alternatively, if your real need is GL account-based substitution/validation during posting, then check whether your scenario can be solved with a Journal Entry Item type context where GL Account is exposed. You would then substitute fields that are available in that context, not necessarily market segment extension fields.

Regarding raising an incident, if your expectation is for SAP to expose GL Account as a selectable precondition in Market Segment context, then that would rather be an enhancement request and not an incident. KBA 2892205 also addresses this boundary.

Did you also consider  GL Account Hierarchies ?

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP Data Warehouse Cloud OData API with PowerBI via a Blank Query
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am getting "The request was rejected because it contained a potentially malicious character" while I am trying to generate access in postman.

Please find screenshot of what all parameter I am passing.

entry.title: Re: I Asked 50 SAP Professionals What Scares Them About AI. Here's What They Said.
entry.content:
Hi MioYasutake

This is a really good question. And it highlights the most common problem with every human-in-the-loop scenario I have seen so far: I have not seen one of them that is a loop!

They are a human in a serial approval (Yes/No) step after the AI. There is:

  • very little reasoning showing how the AI came to its conclusion (this is a huge problem because mostly the AI doesn't know so it can't say)
  • very little the human can do beyond approve or reject
  • no path for the human to feed their reasoning back to the AI so that the AI learns better the context of its recommendations and therefore to make better recommendations.

In other words, it is not a loop. The term is mostly a comfort blanket for marketing purposes, so people (customers) will trust the decisions made behind boundaries they cannot see through.

It is covering one bad situation by making another one on top of it.

This is not to say that there are no loops - just that I haven't seen any yet.

If we want to have entry-level people in our business who can learn and perform to the standard we aspire to, we have to provide them with the information context to learn and improve. We have to build real actual functioning loops, and not talk about them or mis-label things that are not loops.

Hope that helps to answer your question.

Cheers
Denver

entry.title: Re: Change of declarant for VAT reporting
entry.content: Hello @PlamenTraychev, I can see it. Is it still not available?

entry.title: ❗ Unable to Deploy MDK Project to Cloud Foundry – html5-push Fails (Service Instance Not Found)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m facing an issue while deploying my MDK project to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry. The deployment fails during the html5-push step with the following error:

Executing task: node /extbin/local/openvscode-server/extensions/mdk-vsc-wing-25.9.12/res/scripts/deployweb.js {"application":{"name":"CDPLIndent","basePath":"/home/user/projects/CDPLIndent_Web","fullPath":"/home/user/projects/CDPLIndent_Web/Application.app","workspacePath":"/home/user/projects"}} 

MTA Build path:/home/user/projects/WebApplicationFactory
start web deploy...
cf env nsCDPLIndentQA-approuter | grep SAP_ROUTER_VERSION
SAP_ROUTER_VERSION: 21

cd "/home/user/projects/WebApplicationFactory/MDKModule" && cf html5-push -n nsCDPLIndentQA_html_repo_host
FAILED
Service instance with name 'nsCDPLIndentQA_html_repo_host' not found

Command failed: cd "/home/user/projects/WebApplicationFactory/MDKModule" && cf html5-push -n nsCDPLIndentQA_html_repo_host

🧩 What I have already checked:

  • The CF CLI is logged into the correct subaccount and space.
  • The approuter app (nsCDPLIndentQA-approuter) is deployed and running.
Has anyone faced this specific Service instance not found error during MDK deployment?
What was the fix?

📌 Additional Info

  • MDK plugin version: 25.9.12
  • Deployment target: Cloud Foundry (SAP BTP)

Any guidance or similar experiences will be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

entry.content:

During my initial configuration step for binding the hana instance i had encounter the error stating: "self-signed certificate in certificate chain". I was able to bundle up the certificates and the binding was successful. After that when i tried with the command "cds deploy --to hana" i have got the error as bellow:

Could anyone help in the resolving of the issue.

Regards,

Babita

entry.title: Re: SAP FI/HR Elster Business Connector
entry.content: Hallo Zusammen, der Business Connector wird ja jetzt zum 31.12.2027 oder 31.12.2030 abgekündigt. Weiß jemand das genaue Datum. Was sind mittlerweile die Alternativen dazu? Viele Grüße, Herbert

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
@qmacro 

I completely understand. I've also noticed that the team is too small to complete all the necessary tasks.
You're not the first to suggest this, either: Jerry has already written something about it.
Specifying what should and shouldn't be reported is a sensible short-term solution, but in the long term, more resources are needed.
In the long term, the problem simply has to be solved by allocating more resources. This brings me back to the point that the SAP community is not receiving the strategic support it should be. This leaves me feeling pessimistic, because the community is so important, yet so chronically neglected that it simply doesn't allow for any major leaps forward.

entry.title: Re: Change of declarant for VAT reporting
entry.content: Hello Chris, now it's OK.

entry.title: Re: Unearthing Efficiencies in Acquire to Decommission process with S/4HANA and Signavio Process Min
entry.content:
Cycle time of Maint. Orders are very difficult to compare with each-other because of each Job can be more or less complex, a repair of an machine could could take some days, if there is a huge damage.

But part cycle times between (final) confirmation and tech. completion (TECO), and settlement of Maint. Orders as far as financial completion (CLSD) could be easier to compare.

entry.title: Advance and Accelerate your Mobile Workflows with Embedded AI
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced digital landscape, embedded AI is revolutionizing mobile workflows by bringing intelligence directly into applications. This integration enables faster decision-making, smarter actions, and seamless productivity without the need to switch contexts. Here are several ways embedded AI is transforming mobile experiences:

  • Delivering contextual assistance at the moment of need
    Embedded AI offers timely support precisely when users require it, enhancing decision-making processes directly within the app.
  • Automating repetitive tasks to boost efficiency
    By automating routine tasks, AI frees up time and resources, allowing users to focus on more strategic activities.
  • Unlocking organizational knowledge for faster and smarter decisions
    AI leverages existing organizational data to provide insights that enable quicker and well-informed decision-making.
  • Empowering users on-the-go with real-time, intelligent insights
    Mobile users receive dynamic and contextually relevant information, which enhances their ability to act swiftly and effectively.
  • Transforming mobile apps into proactive productivity tools
    With embedded AI, mobile applications become active parts in driving productivity.

 

UX Components and Patterns for Enhanced Clarity, Trust, and Usability

To ensure the smooth integration of AI into mobile workflows, we have designed UX components and patterns that prioritize clarity, trust, and usability. These UI elements are thoughtfully crafted to embed intelligence directly into applications, thereby enriching the user experience.

AI Notice

The AI notice feature transparently communicates when and how AI is being used within an app, building trust with users by keeping them informed. It is applied on components or pages that contain AI-generated fields, appearing interactively beneath each field. Users can tap a hyperlink within the notice to access additional information about the AI technology used to generate the content, including data usage, privacy, and potential risks.

Learn more about the AI notice pattern in our design guidelines.

AI notice examples

 AI Button

The AI button acts as an entry point for initiating AI-driven actions, enabling users to interact with AI seamlessly. It allows users to activate AI-powered features within a user flow, with the AI icon indicating where such features are available. Tapping the "Generate" button starts the content generation process. During this process, the button updates to "Stop Generating," giving users the option to pause the process at any moment. Once the generation is complete or stopped, the button changes to a "Revise" button, allowing users to make adjustments or regenerate content.

Check out AI buttons to get more details.

AI buttons

AI Acknowledgement

AI acknowledgement ensures informed user consent, capturing and recording acceptance of AI functionalities. It delivers crucial information about AI services to users in a standardized manner, focusing on updates, data privacy, and potential risks, thereby establishing clear user expectations and fostering trust. To keep users informed about any AI service updates, contextual hints are recommended on the settings page, allowing users to review changes. Within the settings, users can also choose to opt out of in-app AI features and data collection. Depending on the definition of opting out, users may disable AI data collection while still being able to use AI features.

For more information, refer to AI Acknowledgement.

AI acknowledgement screens

AI Progress Indicators

AI progress indicators manage user expectations by visually displaying the status during AI processing, ensuring users know their request is actively being processed. Once content generation begins, the indicator changes its state to "processing" or "loading," persisting until the task is completed or stopped. The indicator appears one second after the process starts and remains visible for at least 1000ms to prevent flickering, with pulses between animation loops. Offering users the option to stop the AI generation process is recommended for greater control.

Learn more about AI Progress Indicators.

AI progress indicators

AI Writing Assistant

The AI writing assistant supports content creation by providing contextual help right where it’s needed. It efficiently edits text in input fields using prompts displayed in a panel, helping users refine their input and complete writing tasks smoothly. Tapping the "Writing Assistant" button opens the panel above the keyboard, and users can close it by clicking "x" if they haven't yet edited content. After their first edit, navigation changes to "Cancel" and "Done," where "Done" saves changes and "Cancel" offers a confirmation dialog to keep or discard edits. Users can interact with elements outside the panel and are prompted with a dialog if they attempt to switch text fields. Each quick prompt selection updates the text instantly and increases the version number, allowing users to track changes easily.

Check out the AI Writing Assistant design guideline article for mor details.

AI writing assistant

AI User Feedback

The AI user feedback feature gathers user feedback to continually refine and enhance AI quality and functionality. It enables users to provide positive or negative feedback on their experiences with embedded AI, from component-level to page-level interactions. AI feedback can e accessed through automatic pop-ups after AI experiences, in-line thumbs-up or thumbs-down buttons next to components, or through flexible re-entry points like overflow buttons. For positive feedback, users tap the thumbs-up button, triggering a toast message confirming their submission. For negative feedback, users tap the thumbs-down button, with options to provide additional details, requiring a manual tap on "Submit" to finalize. If users reconsider negative feedback, they can change it to positive by tapping the thumbs-up button in the detail view, and the system updates the feedback accordingly.

Find out more about AI User Feedback.

AI user feedback

 

Use Cases - AI Writing Assistant

In the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app, the AI writing assistant is set to serve as a powerful tool for users to efficiently draft goal descriptions. This planned feature will aid users in bypassing the often tedious and error-prone process of composing these descriptions manually. For instance, when a manager needs to set performance goals for their team members, the AI writing assistant can suggest clear and concise descriptions, ensuring alignment with company objectives and language standards. By providing a few keywords or phrases, users will be able to generate well-structured goal descriptions, reducing errors and saving valuable time. This streamlined process promises to enhance productivity while ensuring consistency and clarity in goal-setting across the organization.  

Goal description using the AI writing assistant

 

Embedded AI Resources

For those eager to integrate embedded AI into their mobile workflows, the following resources are now available for Android and iOS: software development kits, comprehensive design guidelines, and Figma UI kits, all crafted to empower you to harness the potential of embedded AI effectively.

Unlock the power of embedded AI and transform your mobile experience today!

Get Started - In-App AI Design Guidelines for SAP Fiori for Android

Get Started - In-App AI Design Guidelines for SAP Fiori for iOS

Figma UI Kit - SAP Fiori for Android

Figma UI Kit - SAP Fiori for iOS

entry.title: Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA: How Three Integrated Capabilities Enable Safe Global Operations
entry.content:
Compliance is no longer just a regulatory necessity, it is a strategic capability.
Across global supply chains, companies must ensure that products are safe, marketable, and transportable at every stage, making product compliance a critical foundation for uninterrupted operations and sustainable growth.
With SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance, companies embed safety, regulatory alignment, and risk mitigation directly into their core business processes.
By uniting Compliance Document Management, Product Marketability, and Dangerous Goods Management in one integrated solution, organizations ensure global readiness, reduce operational risk, and turn compliance into a true business enabler across the entire product lifecycle.

If you would like to learn more about Product Compliance, the blog posts and inbrief videos below explore each of the three main capabilities within SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance in detail, highlighting key functionalities and demonstrating the business value in practice:

Compliance Document Management – Simplifying Global Regulatory Documentation
How SAP S/4HANA automates the creation, management, and distribution of compliance documents such as SDSs and product labels.
- Automated document generation
- Integrated regulatory checks
- Full transparency through analytics

Blog Post & Video:
Simplifying Global Compliance – How SAP S/4HANA Tr... - SAP Community

Product Marketability – Ensuring Global Readiness & Compliance
How companies can evaluate whether their products meet market-specific requirements and remain globally marketable.
- Embedded regulatory content
- Automated compliance checks across the value chain
- Proactive risk management and faster time-to-market

Blog Post & Video:
Rethinking Product Compliance – How SAP S/4HANA He... - SAP Community

Dangerous Goods Management – Safe and Compliant Global Transport
How SAP S/4HANA helps classify, package, and transport hazardous materials safely and efficiently.
- Embedded dangerous goods classifications
- Automated logistics checks preventing non-compliant shipments
- Real-time visibility into transport permissions and restrictions

Blog Post & Video:
Enhancing Safety and Efficiency – How SAP S/4HANA ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Third Party Sales Order Processing in SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello,

I need help with a few items related to third-party and subcontracting sales orders.

Scenario 1:
The client creates a Sales Order. The items to be sold require rework (same material code) through a subcontractor. The client wants a Purchase Requisition (PR) to be created automatically when the Sales Order is created.

Attempt and Issue:
I changed the item category to Bought-in, which successfully created a PR. I then created a Purchase Order (PO) from that PR with account assignment L (Subcontracting). However, the components are not appearing in the subcontracting cockpit.

Scenario 2:
The client is selling air conditioners, but the installation will be handled by a third-party service provider.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: What’s new in DTV 1.7
entry.content:
Hello Abhyarthana,

I like to read your blog about DTV-Tool and I have a technical question about the DTV tool.
I wanted to ask if an older version is used in the ECC version, such as 1.4 and the extraction is made there, then the system is converted to S4 and then the 1.7 Version comes automatically with it. If you want to make the extraction afterwards in the new version, are there technical difficulties because they are two different versions or will that be automatically adjusted?

entry.title: Re: I Asked 50 SAP Professionals What Scares Them About AI. Here's What They Said.
entry.content:
@MioYasutake 

Thanks for this raw and honest take, it's refreshing to see someone call out the real anxieties in the SAP world without sugarcoating. I've been in SAP consulting for about 5 years now, and yeah, that "confidently wrong" AI fear hits home. Last year on a project, we tested an AI tool for config suggestions, and it nailed 80%, but it bombed on compliance checks took us days to clean up. Made me realize governance isn't optional; it's everything.

On the hope side, I'm in the "curious but uncertain" camp from your poll. AI could free us from the grunt work, letting us focus on strategy, but only if we get the data house in order first. What's one small step you've seen work to ease these fears in teams? Would love to hear!

entry.title: Re: Support of a Compact Display for Tables (Live BW)
entry.content:
Hello @Lothar_Koeder , 

Thanks for your kind comment.

We are considering extending Compact Display to MDS databases (including Datasphere) in the future. This is currently a backlog item, but no ETA has been assigned yet.

In the meantime, please feel free to continue voting for the existing Influence request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/232296 . Alternatively, you may also create a dedicated request.

Thanks and best regards.

GB

entry.title: Re: Australia Localization for S/4 HANA Private Cloud 202...
entry.content: Many thanks, Lakshmipathi. That certainly sounds like the right approach, particularly given that OTXAU has been enhanced compared to the longstanding TAXAU procedure. To activate the Australian localisation scope items in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, should we be using the Manage Your Solution (F2196) app? I do not currently have access to this app and would need to request the Basis team to enable it. I do, however, have access to review the Solution Builder in our Sandpit system (transaction /SMB/BBI). I can see available scope items for Austria (e.g. AT_16R_OP – Bank Integration with Multibank Connectivity), Germany, and the United States — but not Australia. We would therefore need to activate the relevant Australian (AU) scope items. From the Solution Builder documentation, it appears that the Basis team would typically import the country-specific content from SAP Best Practices. However, Best Practices content is now surfaced via Signavio Process Navigator. Within Signavio, I can see numerous SAP Best Practices that can be filtered by region (e.g. AU), but I do not see what appears to be a distinct “localisation pack” download. Could you please advise where the Australian localisation content for Private Cloud is sourced from? Is it activated exclusively via the Manage Your Solution app? Or is there a separate country version / localisation package that must be installed by Basis before the AU scope items become available in /SMB/BBI? Kind regards, Adrian

entry.title: Re: Erro message 'Wrong order of calls <- CALL opendir
entry.content: how do you resolve the issue ? using above note or something else i am facing same issue.

entry.title: SBO Mailer Test Connection Failed – Server Unreachable (SAP B1 10 FP2508)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m trying to configure the SBO Mailer in SAP Business One 10 FP2508.

In the System Landscape Directory, I go to Job Service Configuration and enter the SMTP server, username, password, and port as required. However, when I run the test, I receive the following error:

Test result: Failed
Message: Test connection failed
Trace: Server unreachable or incorrect

The SMTP server is working normally, and the same email account is functioning correctly outside of SAP.

Is there something I might be missing in the configuration?

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Automated Trace Enabler in SAP CPI
entry.content:
@Daggolu_PremSai Great work !

One thing that sets this solution apart from browser extensions is the ability to schedule a trace. In rare cases, logging info in custom headers & attachments don't give you the full story. Scheduled trace can be a lifesaver for those 'random' bugs that happen when you aren't online.

br

Mateusz

entry.title: Re: Implementation and Customizing of Real-Time Instant P...
entry.content: are these the correct notes?

entry.title: transitive dependencies between extensions in SAP Commerce
entry.content:
Hi there!I would like to clarify a question regarding transitive dependencies between extensions in SAP Commerce (Hybris).

Suppose we have the following setup:

The customcore extension depends on customintegration:
<requires-extension name="customintegration"/>

The customstorefront extension depends on customcore:
<requires-extension name="customcore"/>

In this case, does it mean that customstorefront indirectly or implicitly depends on customintegration as well?

In other words, are transitive dependencies automatically resolved between extensions in Hybris?

Thanks in advance for your answers!

entry.title: Re: C_S4CFi- Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Clou...
entry.content: Hey Gabriela, Thank you a lot for your detailed respond and Congratulations for your new certification !

entry.title: Re: Datasphare: Text doesnt show when using associated di...
entry.content:
Quick update, 

it was a bug that is solved with new 2026.04 DSP update.

entry.title: Re: Datasphare: Text doesnt show when using associated di...
entry.content:
This is solved with the release 2026.4 - so the dimensions added by reference in the Analytic Model are now behaving as expected (including their texts).

Great that you've pointed it out @ejke

entry.title: Intercompany Order-Related Billing (Output document RDIV/report RVIVAUFT) -Vendor Invoice Generation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are using Advanced IC Sales functionality (goods are being sold to consumer through selling company with delivery done by delivering company). As a part of standard process we are generating IC customer billing document (in delivering company) and IC vendor invoice (in selling company).

Sometimes we need to perform customer refund (selling company customer cedit memo) with its re-issuing to delivering company as intercompany credit memo. 

The standard solution supports RDIV (report RVIVAUFT) output document for billing, which triggers IC invoice/credit memo. It works fine - IGA document was generated and released in FI, but it represents only 1 side of operation (customer) - to finish the process I need to post respective vendor credit memo in 2nd company.

I tried using RD04 output, but it works only if purchase order is in place - for such refund process they are not in use.

What is the way I can trigger and generate vendor credit memo after RDIV document processing?

entry.title: Re: Update before current payroll period is not allowed
entry.content:
Hi Giorgio,

Thanks for reply

Link u shared its not opening.
I tried your suggestion, to not change dates, This time i m not getting any error
 but old dates coming as begda and endda coming as payroll control record's begda.,
so endda should be 999931231

Regards
Vivek

entry.title: Datashpere Local Table "insufficient privilege"
entry.content:
Hello,

We have created from the database explorer, with a specific database user a table. The data in that table is added by a Integration Suite process, working fine.

I created a DS table definition refering this HANA table (but in the DS DB)

When trying to read that data there is no problem, but now a would like to move that table to a newer space, just followed the same process but we wanted to copy the data from the old table to the new, just with a share from the old space to the new and a data flow.

But, I'm getting this error

DBS-070407: |Loader LoadMapper_ceadfe63-ecac-4390-9fec-e5e58fd49640_target1_1TL_OneS_EXCHANGERATES SQL submitted to database <> resulted in error <General error;258 insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found with guid '8378B4CB2A342A41BE3EDE38A5675F38'>. The SQL submitted is <TRUNCATE TABLE "ODS_PRD"."1TL_OneS_EXCHANGERATES" >. RUN-051005: |Loader LoadMapper_ceadfe63-ecac-4390-9fec-e5e58fd49640_target1_1TL_OneS_EXCHANGERATES Execution of <Clean Table Before Load> for target <1TL_OneS_EXCHANGERATES> failed. Possible causes: (1) Error in the SQL syntax; (2) Database connection is broken; (3) Database related errors such as transaction log is full, etc.; (4) The user defined in the datastore has insufficient privileges to execute the SQL. If the error is for preload or postload operation, or if it is for regular load operation and load triggers are defined, check the SQL. Otherwise, for (3) and (4), contact your local DBA.DBS-070407: |Loader LoadMapper_ceadfe63-ecac-4390-9fec-e5e58fd49640_target1_1TL_OneS_EXCHANGERATES SQL submitted to database <> resulted in error <General error;258 insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found with guid '8378B4CB2A342A41BE3EDE38A5675F38'>. The SQL submitted is <TRUNCATE TABLE "ODS_PRD"."1TL_OneS_EXCHANGERATES" >.|<Clean Table Before Load>|<1TL_OneS_EXCHANGERATES>

 

So it seems that the normal DS user does not have enought priviliges on that table, but how could I set this and where should I set this.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Connector test in integration suite fails
entry.content:
Hi Rahul,

Apologies, I should have updated this ticket.   This issue was caused by a firewall block on the route back,   we had to have the network team build a new route in the firewall for us.   We suffered this for ages until we had nowhere else to look apart from the network team who traced and found the needed network rule change very quickly.

Hope it works.

Many Thanks

Alex

entry.title: Re: Cost center MIS not available in system; manual Excel...
entry.content:
Hello @S0027059524 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you are correct. A financial Statement Version is a GL account hierarchy used for financial statement presentation. You cannot add a CO dimension like Cost Center as an additional grouping level inside the same FSV structure.

Question: Is there any standard capability in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to enable Cost Center based grouping within an FSV structure?

Answer:

  • Cost center based grouping inside an FSV is not supported as a standard behaviour.
  • FSV like reporting with Cost Center drilldown is only supported when you use Embedded Analytics (Custom Analytical Queries, Manage KPIs and Reports and standard cost center apps), and optionally you may also use SAC for management reporting.

The following references provides some insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/8e040a45e4db4e6abd426bee0a86dc8a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/c00cbf7fe8464663aee830fb6e7eec13.html

A Public Cloud, clean core best practice is to use a GL account hierarchy (your FSV) plus Cost Center as a characteristic in an analytical report.

Alternatively, if the ask is mainly from the P&L department, start with the delivered cost center analytics (Cost Centers Review Booklet) and cost accounting line item app (Display Line Items Cost Accounting), then align the layout to your business needs.

For more details, consult https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/95063eff5ee0467f8cc04b4ff8f8ca01.html

If the business wants an MIS pack that looks like an FSV that has multiple pages, commentary, scheduling and distribution, then the usual best practice is SAC consuming your S4H analytical query, your GL hierarchy and cost center hierarchy. See Custom Analytical Queries and KBA 2710858 for more insights.

In summary,

  • FSV is a GL account hierarchy and does not supporting adding cost center as an additional grouping level inside the FSV itself.
  • In Public Cloud, the best practice for an FSV like MIS with cost center drilldown is Embedded Analytics; where you maintain the GL hierarchy in Manage Global Hierarchies, build a Custom Analytical Query that includes GL account and cost center, and publish the statement style report in Manage KPIs and reports.
  • For executive packs, consume the same query in SAC.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP TM Transportation Allocation - Carrier 1 in the morning Carrier 2 in the afternoon
entry.content:
Dear TM Experts,

I am struggeling with the transportation allocation set up.

  • Carrier 1 (2010054) drives between 3am and 1pm.
  • Carrier 2 (2010051) drives between 2pm and 11pm.

I have therefore set up transportation allocation and changed the carrier selection settings in the planning profile to "use transportation allocation".

Nevertheless, it always picks 2010051, and gives this carrier 2 priority, although there is no priority given in the transportation lane. Any hint?

There are no good documentations found for transportation allocation, I assume there are not a lot of customers using it.

---

Set Up:

Carrier Selection Setting, see attachment 1

Transportation Allocation, see attachment 2

Transportation Lane, see attachment 3

SE38: /SCMTMS/TSPS_OPT_BGD, see attachment 4

I execute the tsps report in order to check the results - and the result is that in both cases it picks Carrier 2 (2010051) - why??! 

FO 6100336289 departure details:  03.03.2026 // 10:00 and arrival details: 03.03.2026 // 10:00

Thanks a lot in advance

Elena

check the attachments

entry.title: Re: Material Description 2
entry.content:
Hi  Ravi,
About material description data exactly we have 2 description but what is the difference between it.

in table MAKT u can see 2 description and the Purpose of them:

MAKTX is typically used for printing/displaying, while MAKTG is used for search help to avoid issues with inconsistent letter casing

  • MAKTX (Material Description): Stores the text as it is entered, supporting both upper and lower case letters.
  • MAKTG (Material Description in Upper Case): Automatically stores the uppercase version of MAKTX It is primarily used for consistent search/match code functionality.
  • I hope this help u
  • Regards.
  • Osama Selim

entry.title: Re: Cost center MIS not available in system; manual Excel reporting delays month-end closure.
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC Cloud (ECC)
entry.content:
TLDR: Change service plan to Test and make sure Communication setting is configured correctly
We experienced the same issue, but have just resolved the issue.
We had a mismatch of our DRC Service Plan/Communication Setting. 
We wanted to set up a Test-system to send Customer Invoices from the business system through DRC, where we encountered the IFLOW_HTTP_COMMAND error. When we encountered the error, we had the service plan, Standard, and had set our Communication Settings to Test. 
We resolved it by changing the DRC service plan to Test aswell, which ultimately led to us being able to getting an approval from KSeF. However, currently we still experience some 403-error on the DRC error log.

entry.title: Accessing fields in RAP app controller using OData (V4)
entry.content:
Hi Developers,

Sorry if this is an easy one but I have not done this before so I am a bit stuck.

I have made a RAP application using Eclipse and Business Application Studio. I am generating a Fiori List Report using OData V4 and the data is passing through fine.

I now want to customise the header using an extension point. I have also completed this with static text as seen below.

 


The problem is, I want to be able to display the data that is in the list but in the header instead where I can perform calculations.

For example let's say the list shows multiple entries of employees and a field is the number of days left of annual leave. In the header I want to be able to calculate and display the total number of days of annual leave remaining for all employees.

I have currently setup a controller and I'm attempting to try and get the data in section from the hook

onAfterRendering. I have explored developer tools but I cant seem to find anything.
 
If you know how to do this, please add as much detail as you can.
 
Thank you very much!
OData 
#

entry.title: Re: Support of a Compact Display for Tables (Live BW)
entry.content:
@GregBotticchio Fantastic that this is finally delivered!
Please now prioritize this soon for Datasphere/SAC MDS based models, so customers are able to start migrating reports.

entry.title: Migrating from Accelerator to Composable Storefront: A Technical Roadmap for Enterprises
entry.content:
If you're reading this, you're probably wondering whether it's time to move from your SAP Commerce Accelerator to Composable Storefront. Maybe your front-end team is frustrated with JSP templates, or you need better mobile experience. Let me walk you through what this migration actually looks like, based on real experience.

Why Migrate?

What you're leaving behind:

  • Server-side JSP rendering
  • Tightly coupled front-end and back-end
  • Limited mobile experience

What you're getting:

  • Modern Angular-based SPA
  • Decoupled architecture via OCC APIs
  • Better performance with SSR
  • Independent front-end deployment

The catch? You're moving from monolithic to microservices-style architecture. Your team needs JavaScript/TypeScript skills.

Step 1: Assessment

Start by cataloging your customizations:

# Find custom JSP files
find web/webroot/WEB-INF/views -name "*.jsp" | grep -v "\/yacceleratorstorefront\/"

# Custom controllers
grep -r "@Controller" --include="*Controller.java" | grep -v "de.hybris.platform"

Create a spreadsheet with every customization. This becomes your migration backlog.

Step 2: Build OCC Foundation

You need to expose everything via OCC APIs first. Here's a quick example for custom product data:

// Custom WsDTO
@ApiModel(value = "customProduct")
public class CustomProductWsDTO extends ProductWsDTO {
    private String warrantyInfo;
    private String installationGuideUrl;
    // getters/setters
}

// Populator
public class CustomProductPopulator implements Populator<ProductModel, CustomProductWsDTO> {
    @Override
    public void populate(ProductModel source, CustomProductWsDTO target) {
        target.setWarrantyInfo(source.getWarrantyInfo());
        target.setInstallationGuideUrl(source.getInstallationGuideUrl());
    }
}

For custom business logic, create OCC endpoints:

@Controller
@RequestMapping(value = "/{baseSiteId}/custom")
public class CustomOccController {
    
    @GetMapping("/products/{productCode}/compatibility")
    @ResponseBody
    public CompatibilityWsDTO checkCompatibility(
            @PathVariable String productCode,
            @RequestParam String customerProductId) {
        
        CompatibilityData data = customProductFacade
            .checkProductCompatibility(productCode, customerProductId);
        return dataMapper.map(data, CompatibilityWsDTO.class);
    }
}

Step 3: Setup Composable Storefront

# Install and setup
ng new my-storefront
cd my-storefront
ng add @spartacus/schematics --base-site=electronics --base-url=https://localhost:9002

Configure your endpoints:

// spartacus-configuration.module.ts
provideConfig({
  backend: {
    occ: {
      baseUrl: environment.occBaseUrl,
      prefix: '/occ/v2/',
      endpoints: {
        product: 'products/${productCode}?fields=DEFAULT,warrantyInfo',
        productCompatibility: 'custom/products/${productCode}/compatibility'
      }
    }
  },
  context: {
    baseSite: ['electronics'],
    currency: ['USD'],
    language: ['en']
  }
})

Step 4: Migrate Components

Here's a real migration example.

Old Accelerator JSP:

<div class="product-detail">
    <h1>${product.name}</h1>
    <div class="warranty-info">
        <strong>Warranty:</strong> ${product.warrantyInfo}
    </div>
</div>

New Angular Component:

// custom-product-detail.component.ts
@Component({
  selector: 'app-custom-product-detail',
  templateUrl: './custom-product-detail.component.html'
})
export class CustomProductDetailComponent implements OnInit {
  product$: Observable<CustomProduct>;
  
  constructor(
    private currentProductService: CurrentProductService,
    private customProductService: CustomProductService
  ) {}
  
  ngOnInit(): void {
    this.product$ = this.customProductService.getCustomProduct();
  }
}
<!-- custom-product-detail.component.html -->
<div *ngIf="product$ | async as product">
  <h1>{{ product.name }}</h1>
  <div *ngIf="product.warrantyInfo">
    <strong>{{ 'product.warranty' | cxTranslate }}:</strong>
    <p>{{ product.warrantyInfo }}</p>
  </div>
</div>

Replace default Spartacus components via config:

provideConfig({
  cmsComponents: {
    ProductDetailsTabComponent: {
      component: CustomProductDetailComponent
    }
  }
})

Step 5: Checkout

Adding custom checkout steps is straightforward:

// Custom step configuration
export const customCheckoutSteps = [
  {
    id: 'deliveryAddress',
    name: 'checkoutProgress.deliveryAddress',
    routeName: 'checkoutDeliveryAddress',
    type: [CheckoutStepType.DELIVERY_ADDRESS]
  },
  {
    id: 'giftMessage',  // Your custom step
    name: 'checkoutProgress.giftMessage',
    routeName: 'checkoutGiftMessage',
    type: [CheckoutStepType.PAYMENT_DETAILS]
  },
  // ... more steps
];

Step 6: SSR Setup

ng add @spartacus/setup --ssr

Add caching for better performance:

// server.ts
const cache = new LRU({
  max: 100,
  ttl: 1000 * 60 * 60 // 1 hour
});

app.engine('html', (filePath, options, callback) => {
  const cacheKey = options.req.url;
  const cachedHtml = cache.get(cacheKey);
  
  if (cachedHtml) {
    return callback(null, cachedHtml);
  }
  
  ngExpressEngine({ bootstrap: AppServerModule })(filePath, options, (err, html) => {
    if (!err && html) cache.set(cacheKey, html);
    callback(err, html);
  });
});

Step 7: Deploy

npm run build:ssr

Simple Kubernetes deployment:

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: composable-storefront
spec:
  replicas: 3
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: storefront-ssr
        image: your-registry/composable-storefront:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 4000
        env:
        - name: OCC_BASE_URL
          value: "https://your-commerce-backend.com"

Common Issues & Quick Fixes

CORS errors?

# local.properties
corsfilter.commercewebservices.allowedOrigins=http://localhost:4200

Random logouts?

provideConfig({
  session: { synchronization: true }
})

Translations not working? Structure your i18n files properly:

// assets/i18n/en/product.json
{
  "product": {
    "warranty": "Warranty",
    "downloadGuide": "Download Guide"
  }
}

My Advice

Don't try to migrate everything at once. Start with one section, test thoroughly, then expand. The Composable Storefront is actively maintained by SAP, so you're not just modernizing—you're joining an ecosystem that keeps evolving.

The migration is substantial but worth it. Your team gets modern tooling, faster deployments, and better customer experience.

Resources

  • Composable Storefront Docs
  • OCC API Reference
  • SAP Community

What's been your biggest challenge with this migration? Share in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Quick Start with SAP HANA PAL: Basic Example Implementation
entry.content:
Great introduction! Thank you very much for sharing!
I was really easy to copy/paste your example and get it running with my own source_data! 👍

Just one minor issue:
In the data-definition, you declared a variable "LT_SCALE_INPUT", but in the source code it is then called "LT_SCALING_INPUT". Also, there is no indication, on how this table is filled.
It would probably make it easier to understand, if you could fix hat.

Thanks again!
Best regards,
Raffael

entry.title: Re: Depreciation Key for Mexico
entry.content:
Hi S. Sumana,

If you have doubts our questions on Mexican content in asset accounting, please create an incident on component: FI-LOC-AA.

Best regards,
  Mick

entry.title: Efficient Handover of Recipe Formulations with SAP Integrated Product Development
entry.content:
Welcome to this blog post! In today’s edition, I want to outline how you can efficiently hand over recipe formulations to manufacturing BOMs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD).

No time for reading? Listen in on our latest Meet the Expert session on the topic.

SAP IPD allows production engineers to derive and maintain bills of material (BOMs) from the recipe formulation handover application in SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD) into an external application like SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Learn how to effectively manage product structures, saving material and operational costs in the process!

In the handover scenario we want to look at today, a recipe engineer has created a formulation recipe in SAP IPD and wants to hand it over to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for production. SAP IPD supports the whole process of referencing a source formulation recipe and deriving a manufacturing BOM out of it.

Recipe engineers can use simplified planning options to plan and restructure BOM items, synchronize the target manufacturing BOM and enrich it with relevant details, like subsets of BOM item attributes.

Production engineers can enjoy our user-friendly Change Incorporation feature to compare and incorporate changes initiated in the SAP IPD recipe formulation to the target BOM, including adding new ingredients that were not included before and updating quantities.

Such changes can be made with or without an existing change number, but always ensuring the target BOM reflects the latest recipe. After you have incorporated all changes into the target BOM, the Change Reconciliation icon will let you know in case there are discrepancies between the source and the target panels and provide status information.

Click on the icon to open the Change Reconciliation Workbench and easily reconcile the changes, ensuring the manufacturing BOM remains accurate and up-to-date, for optimum production consistency and quality.

The application also allows you to add certain manufacturing consumables and other essential details to the BOM, such as item texts and scrap material. This enrichment brings the BOM closer to the production-ready status, reducing the transition time and effort from R&D to production and improving efficiency.

Sounds good? Also want to know what’s next for Process Industry customers?

There are good reasons to look forward to our coming releases:

Among the features we plan to bring to you soon are the smart production preparation for target BOMs, AI-based mass creation of handover activities for different plants, improved handover to the master recipe, and the background creation of handover activities. So stay tuned on our Roadmap for more streamlined processes with optimal consistency and quality.

As you can see, we are all set for a successful start to 2026.

For more details about the handover of recipe formulations to manufacturing BOMs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, including FAQs, check out our latest Meet the Expert session on the topic.

More Information / Additional Links 

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts here:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: linkedin.com/in/annette-garcia-lorenzo

entry.title: 🇧🇷 SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications: ML, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs (Ribeirão Preto, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event focuses on SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications, covering topics such as machine learning, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs. You don't need to have any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity and some basic programming skills!

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, April 10, in Ribeirão Preto, Brazil 🇧🇷 from 09:30 to 14:30 local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

The event is at Fatec Ribeirão Preto - Av. Pio XII, 1255 - Vila Virginia, Ribeirão Preto - State of São Paulo, Brazil

Please note that space is limited, as we can only accommodate 25 attendees.

By the end of the session, you’ll have built examples that demonstrate how SAP HANA Cloud can serve as a versatile back-end for modern Artificial Intelligence applications:

  • Leverage the SAP HANA Vector Engine for high‑dimensional embeddings—building a vector store that supports semantic similarity search.
  • Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)  to enrich generative AI outputs with precise, up-to-date data.
  • Model and Query a Knowledge Graph inside SAP HANA using RDF triples to extract semantic relationships.
  • Integrate HANA with LangChain to build AI apps that orchestrate prompt workflows, execute database statements, and navigate knowledge graphs—all directly from Python, in this case.

entry.title: Re: Texts in Purchase Contract Output Form not Displayed
entry.content:
Dear @SauMaris,

The most common miss in your specific case is wrong Text IDs (A** vs K**)

Text IDs with prefix A are typically used for RFQs, while contracts use K**. Community references explicitly describe the convention F = PO, A = RFQ, K = Contract so if you configured A05 / A06 in CBC ID 104689 the output framework will not find matching contract texts and TextNodes stay empty in the form data source

More details below: (I know the user is writing about the Private Cloud version but it is referring to the Public Cloud version, screenshot from my CBC for the Public Cloud version)

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/po-text-id/qaq-p/12768369

Summary:

In CBC 104689 Define Texts for Contract, maintain:

  • Header texts tab:
    • Text object = EKKO
    • Text ID = K05 (Terms of Delivery) / K06 (Shipping Instructions) (not A05/A06)

 

  • Item texts tab:
    • Text object = EKPO
    • relevant K** text IDs used at item level

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: New JavaScript Event in SAP Analytics Cloud End User File Upload
entry.content:
Hey @gerd_schoeffl,

This is a good update,

But, I don't know what is use case of onBeforeExecute, because just get called when you click the Upload Button. It should be called after selecting file would be more helpful.

Example: - If someone want to apply some validation, like i want to show popup message to user after selecting file, and that time I will show this "XYZ ACCOUNT members are not allowed" using Popup. and then there should be one more function called as DataUploadStarter_1.execute().

So in the Popup we will write script on button.

OK, Continue --> DataUploadStarter_1.execute()
CANCEL --> Close the Popup

I know we can do this in onBeforeImport, to skip that account which are not allowed. But user won't know which account are ignored. We can show that in onAfterExecute. But it would be good if we can show some validation or any message before Upload (after file selected).

entry.title: 🇧🇷 SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications: ML, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs (São Leopoldo, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event focuses on SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications, covering topics such as machine learning, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs. You don't need to have any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity and some basic programming skills!

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Monday, April 13, in São Leopoldo, Brazil 🇧🇷 from 09:30 to 14:30 local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

The event is at SAP Labs Latin America (SLE01) - Avenida SAP - Cristo Rei, São Leopoldo - RS, Brazil

Please note that space is limited, as we can only accommodate 30 attendees.

By the end of the session, you’ll have built examples that demonstrate how SAP HANA Cloud can serve as a versatile back-end for modern Artificial Intelligence applications:

  • Leverage the SAP HANA Vector Engine for high‑dimensional embeddings—building a vector store that supports semantic similarity search.
  • Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)  to enrich generative AI outputs with precise, up-to-date data.
  • Model and Query a Knowledge Graph inside SAP HANA using RDF triples to extract semantic relationships.
  • Integrate HANA with LangChain to build AI apps that orchestrate prompt workflows, execute database statements, and navigate knowledge graphs—all directly from Python, in this case.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications: ML, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs (Recife, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event focuses on SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications, covering topics such as machine learning, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs. You don't need to have any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity and some basic programming skills!

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Thursday, April 16, in Recife, Brazil 🇧🇷 from 09:30 to 14:30 local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

The event is at TBD.

Please note that space is limited, as we can only accommodate 30 attendees.

By the end of the session, you’ll have built examples that demonstrate how SAP HANA Cloud can serve as a versatile back-end for modern Artificial Intelligence applications:

  • Leverage the SAP HANA Vector Engine for high‑dimensional embeddings—building a vector store that supports semantic similarity search.
  • Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)  to enrich generative AI outputs with precise, up-to-date data.
  • Model and Query a Knowledge Graph inside SAP HANA using RDF triples to extract semantic relationships.
  • Integrate HANA with LangChain to build AI apps that orchestrate prompt workflows, execute database statements, and navigate knowledge graphs—all directly from Python, in this case.
